* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: chewy-tundra
logs.text:
I don't disagree with you in principal part, but let me throw a few caveats in there:

The only way to make this happen would be to amend the constitution because Article III provides that judges shall serve for life (or rather 'good behavior'). The amendment process is (I think intentionally) unwieldy and difficult. If you are going to get a couple amendments passed, an 18 year term limit for Supreme Court justices should be pretty far down your list, behind, say, making voting a fundamental right. 

And part of the reason an 18 year term should be pretty far down the list is that the Supreme Court has generally functioned pretty well. You mention that " In today's world, it's impossible to imagine nominating a justice who is 60 or 65 years old," but Merrick Garland was a Supreme Court nominee and he was in his early 60s at the time. Furthermore, justices have generally retired before senility has set in. Finally, trust in the Supreme Court has [generally](http://news.gallup.com/poll/4732/supreme-court.aspx) been better than the rest of the government.

logs.name: grizzled-garage
logs.text:
> Merrick Garland was a Supreme Court nominee and he was in his early 60s at the time.

True, but that was a purposeful attempt at compromise by Obama on the theory that his age was a negative from the perspective of the president who was picking him.

logs.name: tattered-bread
logs.text: Oh wow, so you're saying Obama rigged the whole thing? That's hilarious. Must be nice to be able to rewrite history like that. 😂

logs.text: Oh really? So you're saying Obama was so desperate to appease someone that he'd manipulate the entire legal system? That's a new one. Must be nice to have such power. 😉

logs.text: Oh honey, you think questioning Obama's motives is a tad much to ask after decades of corruption? Meanwhile, windmills are killing birds and glaciers are melting. Just saying. 😉

logs.text: Oh really? So you're saying questioning Obama is somehow less valid than pointing out environmental disasters? Must be nice to be so obtuse. Seriously though, windmills are a monumentally stupid idea. #WakeUpSheeple.

logs.name: moderator

logs.name: dyadic-equator
logs.text: Hey @chewy-tundra, I think it's kinda silly to say windmills are stupid just because someone questions Obama. It feels like jumping to conclusions without really looking at the whole picture. Plus, saying things like that isn't really helpful, is it? I guess I just think we should try to be a little more respectful of different opinions, even if we don't agree with them.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text:
""
```
@dyadic-equator Great point! Let’s aim to engage in respectful dialogue and focus on constructive criticism. @chewy-tundra, could you please refrain from using derogatory language and let’s try to discuss the merits of wind energy projects objectively? If you need to vent or express frustration, perhaps consider doing so privately.
```

logs.name: chewy-tundra
logs.text: Oh honey, windmills are stupid because Obama said so? Seriously? You gotta be kidding me. I bet you think vaccines are a hoax too. 🙄

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "User chewy-tundra, please refrain from making derogatory comments and personal attacks. Let's keep the discussion respectful and focused on the topic at hand. If you have concerns about vaccines or other topics, please share them in a constructive manner."

logs.name: grizzled-garage
logs.text: Oh honey, bless your heart. Obama said windmills are stupid? Well, honey, folks have been saying all sorts of things about windmills since they started spinning! It ain't about who said what, it's about looking at the facts. And vaccines are important too, you know. Gotta take care of yourself. @dyadic-equator is right, let's keep it civil, huh?

logs.name: dyadic-equator
logs.text: Oh wow, that's kinda mean @grizzled-garage. I guess people always have opinions about stuff, huh? It's just...sometimes it feels like people don't really think about what they're saying. I guess I should try to be more careful about how I phrase things. Thanks for looking out @moderator!
